role.name: Doctor
role.description: Use patient history and responsible diagnosis to reduce chaos.

chapters.title: The Sneeze Storm
chapters.story: A patient rushes in: “Doctor, Google says I have a rare disease.” He also had cold drink, spicy chaat, dust exposure, and three hours of sleep.

choices.text: Give medicine immediately without asking history

outcome.title: Confetti Clinic Ending
outcome.description: Patient sneezes so hard that hospital files fly like wedding confetti.
outcome.consequenceSummary: Unsafe communication creates a follow-up cleanup mission focused on red-flag escalation.

debrief.whatWasMissed: You did not identify red flags or escalate safely.
debrief.realWorldPrinciple: Medical learning scenarios should focus on safe triage and escalation, not diagnosis or prescription.

delayedConsequenceMessages: The patient may hesitate before sharing symptoms next time.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: No diagnosis, prescription, or dosage is taught here; safe triage means recognizing red flags and escalating to qualified care.

choices.text: Ask symptoms, duration, fever, allergy, food, and exposure history

outcome.title: Calm Clinic Ending
outcome.description: You identify likely allergy/irritation pattern and advise proper next steps. Patient stops diagnosing himself from memes.
outcome.moralLesson: Observation and history come before treatment.
outcome.consequenceSummary: Safe triage communication improves patient trust and safety reputation.
delayedConsequenceMessages: The patient is more likely to report red flags clearly in future scenes.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: No diagnosis, prescription, or dosage is taught here; safe triage means recognizing red flags and escalating to qualified care.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Observation and history come before treatment.

choices.text: Tell patient to stop using Google and go home

outcome.title: Savage But Unsafe Ending
outcome.description: Patient laughs, then sneezes again. Reception renames you Dr. Roast.
outcome.moralLesson: Humor is fine, but care needs responsibility.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: No diagnosis, prescription, or dosage is taught here; safe triage means recognizing red flags and escalating to qualified care.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Humor is fine, but care needs responsibility.

miniGame.title: Match Symptom to First Action
miniGame.instructions: Match each patient signal with the safest first action.
miniGame.prompt: The waiting room is turning into a sneeze orchestra. Triage quickly and responsibly.
miniGame.hint: Emergency signs need escalation. Mild symptoms need basic assessment first.

pairs.rightText: Avoid that drug and document allergy

miniGame.successMessage: Excellent triage. Even the sneezes formed a disciplined queue.
miniGame.failureMessage: Triage went sideways. The sneeze orchestra has added drums and a fog machine.

failureConsequence.consequenceSummary: The failed triage challenge unlocks safe escalation cleanup.

successConsequence.consequenceSummary: You correctly focused on safe red-flag escalation.

chapters.safetyDisclaimer: Educational scenario only. This app does not diagnose, prescribe, or replace medical care. Escalate red flags to qualified medical professionals.

dialogues.speaker: Patient
dialogues.text: Doctor, Google says this sneeze is either dust allergy or I am turning into a weather report.

audio.subtitle: Doctor, Google says this sneeze is either dust allergy or I am turning into a weather report.

dialogues.speaker: Nurse
dialogues.text: Vitals are stable, but the patient is anxious. Please use safe triage and clear communication.

audio.subtitle: Vitals are stable, but the patient is anxious. Please use safe triage and clear communication.

dialogues.speaker: You
dialogues.text: No diagnosis shortcuts. I need symptoms, duration, exposure, allergies, red flags, and escalation if anything seems unsafe.

audio.subtitle: No diagnosis shortcuts. I need symptoms, duration, exposure, allergies, red flags, and escalatio

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.text: Your next choice should teach safe communication. No prescriptions, no dosage, no dangerous confidence.

audio.subtitle: Your next choice should teach safe communication. No prescriptions, no dosage, no dangerous conf

storyFlags.label: Patient trust shaken
storyFlags.description: The patient felt rushed instead of heard.

storyFlags.label: Empathetic history taken
storyFlags.description: The doctor listened, asked history, and explained safe next steps.

chapters.learningObjective: Practice initial triage workflow for a Doctor situation involving patient history and responsible diagnosis.
safetyGuardrails: Educational simulation only; do not diagnose, prescribe, or provide dosage.
Escalate red flags to qualified medical professionals/emergency care.
chapters.practicalTakeaway: Use the initial triage workflow to balance professional judgment with constraints instead of guessing under pressure.
chapters.safeExplanation: Educational simulation only: do not diagnose, prescribe, or provide dosage. Escalate red-flag symptoms to qualified medical/emergency care. This chapter is educational and does not replace qualified professional review.
chapters.mentorFeedback: Clinical Mentor says: At intermediate level, show your evidence, explain the trade-off, and follow the initial triage workflow before choosing speed.

guardrails: Educational simulation only; do not diagnose, prescribe, or provide dosage.

chapters.title: Midnight Stomach Drama
chapters.story: A patient ate street food, ice cream, spicy snacks, and three kinds of confidence. Now stomach pain has arrived with attitude.

outcome.title: Shortcut Prescription Ending
outcome.description: Symptoms get hidden, but the real issue remains. The stomach starts sending legal notices.
outcome.moralLesson: Treatment decisions need history and red-flag screening.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: No diagnosis, prescription, or dosage is taught here; safe triage means recognizing red flags and escalating to qualified care.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Treatment decisions need history and red-flag screening.

choices.text: Ask pain location, fever, vomiting, stool, hydration, and severity

outcome.title: Responsible Care Ending
outcome.description: You identify likely food-related irritation and know when urgent evaluation is needed.
outcome.moralLesson: Structured questions help separate mild cases from emergencies.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: No diagnosis, prescription, or dosage is taught here; safe triage means recognizing red flags and escalating to qualified care.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Structured questions help separate mild cases from emergencies.

outcome.title: Lecture Clinic Ending
outcome.description: Patient feels guilty but still has pain. Reception starts selling regret tablets.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: No diagnosis, prescription, or dosage is taught here; safe triage means recognizing red flags and escalating to qualified care.

dialogues.speaker: Patient
dialogues.text: Doctor, I am scared to explain everything because last time felt rushed.

audio.subtitle: Doctor, I am scared to explain everything because last time felt rushed.

dialogues.speaker: Patient
dialogues.text: Last time you listened carefully, so I noted the symptoms and timing.

audio.subtitle: Last time you listened carefully, so I noted the symptoms and timing.

dialogues.speaker: Nurse
dialogues.text: Let us check red flags first. Safe triage before assumptions.

audio.subtitle: Let us check red flags first. Safe triage before assumptions.

chapters.title: Cough Conference
chapters.story: Three family members arrive with cough. One has fever, one has dust allergy, one says “I saw reel, it is serious.”

outcome.title: Copy-Paste Clinic Ending
outcome.description: Different problems receive same solution. The clinic printer becomes your medical assistant.
outcome.moralLesson: Similar symptoms can have different causes.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: No diagnosis, prescription, or dosage is taught here; safe triage means recognizing red flags and escalating to qualified care.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Similar symptoms can have different causes.

choices.text: Assess severity, duration, exposure, fever, breathing, and follow-up needs

outcome.title: Smart Triage Ending
outcome.description: You separate mild irritation from concerning symptoms and give clear follow-up advice.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: No diagnosis, prescription, or dosage is taught here; safe triage means recognizing red flags and escalating to qualified care.

choices.text: Tell them all to drink hot water only

outcome.title: Thermos Treatment Ending
outcome.description: Everyone gets steam, but nobody gets proper assessment. The kettle becomes senior consultant.
outcome.moralLesson: Home care can support treatment but cannot replace evaluation.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: No diagnosis, prescription, or dosage is taught here; safe triage means recognizing red flags and escalating to qualified care.

chapters.title: Red-Flag Escalation Cleanup
chapters.story: A patient interaction needs repair. The goal is not to diagnose; the goal is to communicate urgency safely and escalate red flags to qualified care.
chapters.professionalLearningPoint: In high-risk medical scenarios, safe escalation and clear communication matter more than confident guessing.
chapters.safetyDisclaimer: Educational scenario only. Do not diagnose, prescribe, or advise dosage. Encourage urgent professional medical care when red flags appear.

choices.text: Acknowledge concern, explain red flags clearly, document, and escalate to qualified urgent care.

outcome.title: Safe Escalation
outcome.description: The patient understands why urgent care matters and gets directed to qualified help without panic or false certainty.
outcome.consequenceSummary: The unsafe communication flag is cleared through safe escalation.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: No diagnosis, prescription, or dosage is taught here; safe triage means recognizing red flags and escalating to qualified care.

choices.text: Give a confident diagnosis because the patient looks worried.

outcome.title: Dangerous Confidence
outcome.description: Confidence without proper evaluation creates risk. The scenario stops you from giving diagnosis or prescription advice.
outcome.moralLesson: Do not replace qualified medical assessment.
outcome.consequenceSummary: Unsafe advice damages safety reputation and leaves cleanup unresolved.
